data.title: Zscaler gets upgrade at BofA after "healthy" quarterly results
data.description:
BofA upgraded Zscaler's (NASDAQ:ZS) rating to Buy from Neutral noting that the cloud security provider's fiscal second-quarter was "healthy" with improvement in upsell capabilities.

The firm raised the price target on the stock to $240 from $215.

On Wednesday, Zscaler raised its fiscal year outlook and the company's second-quarter results beat estimates, which saw largely bullish views from Wall Street analysts.

BofA said Zscaler had strong results with billings/revenue up 18%/23% year-over-year, above Street’s estimate of 15%/21%, driven by strong upsell and larger deal sizes.

The analysts noted that key positives include a strengthening portfolio, especially on zero-trust and data initiatives, and a ramp in sales productivity, following last year's changes.

The analysts added that, as the company is showing good signs of successful diversification, solid order growth and improvement in upsell capabilities, they upgraded the stock to Buy from Neutral and increased their price target.

Shares of Zscaler rose about 1% premarket on Friday.

data.title: MDA Space Non-GAAP EPS of C$0.28, revenue of C$346.6M; initiates FY25 outlook
data.description:
• MDA Space press release (OTCPK:MDALF): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of C$0.28.
  • Revenue of C$346.6M.
  • Backlog of C$4.4 billion at quarter-end, up 42% Y/Y.
  • Adjusted EBITDA of C$71 million, up 68% Y/Y; adjusted EBITDA margin of 20.5%.
  • Introduction of 2025 Financial Outlook
    • Revenues expected to be C$1.50 - C$1.65 billion, representing ~ 45% Y/Y growth.
    • Adjusted EBITDA expected to be C$290 - C$320 million, representing ~ 40% Y/Y growth, with adjusted EBITDA margin of 19%-20%.

data.title: Ensign Energy Services GAAP EPS of -C$0.11, revenue of C$426.52M; initiates FY25 outlook
data.description:
• Ensign Energy Services press release (OTCPK:ESVIF): Q4 GAAP EPS of -C$0.11.
  • Revenue of C$426.52M (-0.9% Y/Y).
  • Adjusted EBITDA for 2024 was C$450.1 million, an eight percent decrease from Adjusted EBITDA of C$490.2 million for 2023.
  • Funds flow from operations for 2024 decreased six percent to C$436.2 million from C$464.9 million in the prior year.
  • The Company has budgeted maintenance capital expenditures for 2025 of approximately C$164.0 million and selective growth and customer funded capital of C$8.0 million. The Company may continue to consider rig relocation, upgrade, or growth projects in response to customer demand and under appropriate contract terms.

data.title: AltaGas Non-GAAP EPS of C$0.76, revenue of C$3.26B; initiates FY25 outlook
data.description:
• AltaGas press release (OTCPK:ATGFF): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of C$0.76.
  • Revenue of C$3.26B (-0.9% Y/Y).
  • Normalized EBITDA1 was C$520 million in the fourth quarter.
  • Normalized FFO was C$397 million or C$1.33 per share for the fourth quarter of 2024, compared to C$376 million or C$1.33 per share for the same quarter of 2023.
  • Cash from operations for the fourth quarter of 2024 was C$508 million or C$1.70 per share, compared to C$154 million or C$0.54 per share for the same quarter of 2023.
  • Outlook: AltaGas continues to focus on executing its corporate strategy of building a diversified platform that operates longlife energy infrastructure assets that connect customers and markets and are positioned to provide resilient and growing value for the Company’s stakeholders. AltaGas expects to achieve guidance ranges that were previously disclosed in December 2024, including: • 2025 Normalized EPS guidance of C$2.10 - C$2.30 per share, compared to actual normalized EPS of C$2.18 and GAAP EPS of C$1.95 in 2024; and • 2025 Normalized EBITDA guidance of C$1,775 million - C$1,875 million, compared to actual normalized EBITDA of C$1.77 billion and income before taxes of C$746 million in 2024.

data.title: Second death linked to measles outbreak reported in U.S.
data.description:
• The New Mexico Department of Health (NMDOH) announced late Thursday the death of a person who was later tested positive for measles, marking the second fatality linked to the ongoing outbreak of the virus in the U.S.
  • The resident of Lea County hadn’t been vaccinated against measles and hadn't sought medical care before passing, the agency said, adding that investigations are underway to determine the official cause of death.
  • “We don’t want to see New Mexicans getting sick or dying from measles,” noted Chad Smelser, NMDOH Deputy State Epidemiologist, adding, “The measles-mumps-rubella (MMR) vaccine is the best protection against this serious disease.”
  • Merck (NYSE:MRK) and GSK (NYSE:GSK) market MMR vaccines in the U.S. In late February, the Texas Department of State Health reported the first death related to the current measles outbreak in the U.S.

data.title: Las Vegas Sands' CEO plans to step down in one year
data.description:
Las Vegas Sands (NYSE:LVS) announced that Robert G. Goldstein, its chairman and chief executive officer and long-time senior executive, will transition to the role of senior advisor on March 1, 2026. Goldstein has agreed to serve as a senior advisor through March 2028. In that capacity, Mr. Goldstein will assist management with its government relations activities, efforts to pursue new physical development opportunities and the company's gaming strategies.

During his time as president and chief operating officer of The Venetian and The Palazzo from 1999 to 2010, Goldstein was responsible for many important property milestones, which also had a broader impact on the Las Vegas Strip as well. While the company was expanding its presence in Macao and Las Vegas, Goldstein was also guiding the development of Sands Bethlehem in eastern Pennsylvania.

In late 2014, after having served as president of global gaming operations, Mr. Goldstein was named the company's president and chief operating officer.  When Sheldon Adelson passed away in January 2021, Goldstein was named the company's chairman and chief executive officer.

Shares of Las Vegas Sands (NYSE:LVS) are down 11.4% on a year-to-date basis.

data.title: LiveRamp announces workforce restructuring
data.description:
• LiveRamp (NYSE:RAMP) has announced a workforce restructuring involving around 65 full-time employees, or ~5% of its workforce.
  • The company expects to incur about $6.5M of restructuring and related charges, primarily related to employee severance and benefits, in the fourth quarter of its fiscal year ending March 31, 2025.
  • LiveRamp (NYSE:RAMP) reaffirmed its revenue and adjusted operating income outlook for Q4 and fiscal year ending March 31, but updated GAAP operating income (loss) outlook to ($13.0) million and $5M for Q4 and fiscal year.
  • Shares were up 5% premarket on Friday.

data.title: Nano Labs receives $1M in USDT from unregistered equity sale
data.description:
• Nano Labs (NASDAQ:NA) received $1M equivalent in USDT from previously announced private placement of 913,714 Class A ordinary shares at $6.46/share, for aggregate gross proceeds of ~$5.9M.
  • NA has issued additional 154,799 Class A ordinary shares to the investors of this private placement.

data.title: Advantage Solutions reports Q4 results
data.description:
• Advantage Solutions press release (NASDAQ:ADV): Q4 adj EBITDA of $94.55M
  • Revenue of $892.29M (-10.0% Y/Y) beats by $28.9M.

data.title: European markets dip after a volatile week
data.description:
Update: The revised reflects market movements of 6.45am ET.

London (UKX) -0.45% to 8,644. 

Germany (DAX:IND) -1.46% to 23,035.

France (CAC:IND) -0.95% to 8,119. France imports and exports remained stable in January.

Slovakia’s economy advanced by 1.8% Y/Y in Q4.

Industrial production in Spain shrank 1% Y/Y in January.

Euro Area GDP expands 1.2% Y/Y, beating estimates.

The pan-European Stoxx 600 (STOXX) edged 0.54% lower to 552.9, rounding off a volatile week due to ongoing uncertainty surrounding US trade policy under the Trump administration, which continued to weigh on investor sentiment. Luxury stocks were among the worst performers.

The European Central Bank made the anticipated interest rate cut, but took a more cautious approach to future rate changes while raising its near-term inflation forecasts.

In the bond market, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield was down 2 basis points to 4.26%.

Germany's 10-year yield was down 7 basis points to 2.82%.

UK's 10-year yield was down less than 2 basis points to 4.69%.

Currencies: (EUR:USD) (GBP:USD) (CHF:USD)

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWG), (NYSE:GF), (NYSEARCA:EWI), (NYSEARCA:EWQ), (NASDAQ:FGM), (NASDAQ:DAX), (NYSEARCA:FLGR), (NYSEARCA:FXB), (NYSEARCA:EWU), (NASDAQ:FKU), (BATS:EWUS), (NYSEARCA:FLGB), (NYSEARCA:GREK).
